agent.title: Preferred Agent
agent.description: Choose your AI coding agent and authentication method
agent.agentAndModelDescription: Provider and model for all operations
agent.apiTokenHelper: Saves automatically when you leave the field
agent.hint: Choose which AI coding agent powers your features. Each agent supports different models and capabilities. Authentication is resolved automatically via your active session.

environment.title: IDE & Terminal
environment.description: Configure your default editor, shell, and terminal preferences
environment.failedToSave: Failed to save IDE & Terminal settings
environment.hint: Your preferred editor opens files for review. The shell setting controls generated scripts. The terminal emulator is launched when opening shell sessions from the web UI.

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.description: Configure PR behavior, approval gates, and CI settings
workflow.defaultFastModeDescription: Skip SDLC phases and implement directly from prompt
workflow.autoApprovePlanDescription: Skip manual review of implementation plan
workflow.collectEvidenceDescription: Capture screenshots and artifacts after implementation
workflow.addEvidenceToPrDescription: Include evidence in the pull request body
workflow.pushOnCompleteDescription: Push to remote when implementation finishes
workflow.openPrOnCompleteDescription: Create a pull request when done
workflow.watchCiAfterPushDescription: Monitor CI and auto-fix failures. Disable to avoid rate limits.
workflow.hint: Control how autonomous each feature run is. Auto-approve skips the human review pause at each phase. Push and PR options control what happens after successful implementation.

ci.title: Continuous Integration
ci.description: Limits and timeouts for CI monitoring
ci.maxLogSizeDescription: Truncate CI logs beyond this limit
ci.pollIntervalDescription: How often to check GitHub for CI status updates
ci.hideCiStatusDescription: Hide CI status badges from feature drawer and merge review
ci.hint: When a feature completes, the agent can watch CI and auto-fix failures. These limits prevent runaway retries and control how much log output is sent to the agent for analysis.

stageTimeouts.title: Stage Timeouts
stageTimeouts.description: Maximum execution time per agent stage
stageTimeouts.hint: Each agent has independently configurable stage timeouts. When a stage exceeds its timeout, the agent is terminated. Longer timeouts are useful for complex implementations. Feature agent defaults to 30 minutes per stage. Analyze repository agent defaults to 10 minutes.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Configure notification channels and event preferences
notifications.sectionDescription: How and when you get notified
notifications.hint: In-app toast notifications keep you in the loop. Fine-tune which agent lifecycle events trigger a notification.

featureFlags.title: Feature Flags
featureFlags.skillsDescription: Enable the skills system for agent capabilities
featureFlags.debugDescription: Show debug panels and verbose logging
featureFlags.githubImportDescription: Enable GitHub repository import in the web UI
featureFlags.adoptBranchDescription: Import existing branches as tracked features
featureFlags.gitRebaseSyncDescription: Enable git rebase-on-main and sync-main operations
featureFlags.reactFileManagerDescription: Use the built-in React file manager instead of the native OS folder picker
featureFlags.inventoryDescription: Enable the Inventory page showing all repositories and features in a tree view
featureFlags.hint: Experimental features that are still under development. Enable at your own risk — they may change or be removed in future versions. Debug mode adds verbose logging useful for troubleshooting.

interactiveAgent.title: Interactive Agent
interactiveAgent.description: Chat tab settings for per-feature interactive agent sessions
interactiveAgent.enableChatTabDescription: Show the Chat tab on all feature detail pages
interactiveAgent.autoTimeoutDescription: Minutes of inactivity before the agent is stopped automatically (1–120)
interactiveAgent.maxConcurrentSessionsDescription: Maximum number of active interactive agent sessions at once (1–10)
interactiveAgent.hint: The Chat tab spawns a persistent agent process per feature. Auto-timeout stops idle sessions automatically to conserve resources. The concurrent session cap prevents runaway resource usage on developer machines.

fabLayout.title: Layout
fabLayout.description: Control the position of floating action buttons in the control center
fabLayout.swapPositionDescription: Move the Chat button to the left and the Create button to the right
fabLayout.hint: The two floating action buttons (Create and Chat) sit in the bottom corners of the control center. Enable this toggle to swap their positions.

database.title: Database
database.locationDescription: Path to the local SQLite database
database.hint: All settings are stored in a local SQLite database at ~/.shep/data. The database uses a singleton record pattern with automatic migrations on startup.

deleteFeature.title: Delete feature?
deleteFeature.description: This will permanently delete {{featureName}} ({{featureId}}). This action cannot be undone.

rejectFeedback.defaultDescription: Provide feedback for the agent to address in the next iteration. Feedback is required.

emptyState.addProjectDescription: Add your project folder to unlock feature creation.
emptyState.addProjectDescriptionLine2: Describe what you need — Shep handles the rest.
emptyState.folderHint: Any folder works — git will be initialized automatically if needed.

welcome.selectAgentSubtitle: Select the AI coding agent you want Shep to use.
welcome.ghCliNotice: <0>GitHub CLI (gh)</0> is required for full CI/CD self-healing capabilities. <1>Install it here</1>.
welcome.ghCliRequiredText: is required for full CI/CD self-healing capabilities.

canvas.noFeaturesDescription: Get started by creating your first feature.

featureNode.archiveConfirmDescription: {{name}} will be hidden from the canvas. You can unarchive it later to restore it.

repositoryNode.removeConfirmDescription: This will remove {{name}} and all its features from your workspace. The repository files on disk won't be affected.

createDrawer.title: NEW FEATURE
createDrawer.featurePlaceholder: e.g. Add GitHub OAuth login with callback handling and token refresh...
createDrawer.approveDescription: Auto-approve phase transitions without manual review.
createDrawer.evidenceDescription: Collect and attach evidence after implementation.
createDrawer.collectDescription: Capture screenshots and artifacts after implementation.
createDrawer.requiresEvidence: Requires evidence collection to be enabled
createDrawer.addToPrDescription: Include evidence in the pull request body.
createDrawer.pushDescription: Push branch to remote after implementation.
createDrawer.prDescription: Open a pull request after pushing.
createDrawer.watchDescription: Watch CI and auto-fix after push.
createDrawer.syncDescription: Pull latest main from remote before creating the feature branch.
createDrawer.pendingModeDescription: Create without starting — start manually later.
createDrawer.fastModeDescription: Skip SDLC phases and implement directly from your prompt.

featureDrawer.excessiveIterations: Iteration {{n}} — consider approving or adjusting feedback to avoid excessive iterations

githubImport.title: Import from GitHub
githubImport.description: Clone a GitHub repository and add it to Shep.

modelPicker.searchPlaceholder: Search or type a model ID…
